I find the Japanese culture very different than the Chinese culture. We enjoy yum cha whereas they enjoy drinking green tea at bars, soaking up the atmosphere rather than at home. My Japanese friend Isaac is an Aquarius while his girlfriend is a Pisces. He's faithful to her and would never cheat on her. When he broke up with his ex, he was pushed over the edge. Even when a stranger talked to him, there was a definite edge. But as soon as he had a new relationship target, it took the edge of his emotional upset. His ex is a swimming champion who far outshines using any swimming styles. She's tall and lean who certainly has the edge over lots of swimmers. Recently, he got into a brawl with a guy who was hitting on his girlfriend. The police showed up later and assigned jealousy as the motive fir the brawl. Last Tuesday, they dined in a restaurant in a hotel which is midway between the past office and his place. They lined up for almost an hour outside the restaurant. The line was so huge that it stretched from the counter through the lobby area and out the main entrance of the hotel. A stout man tried to push in but was caught. Last Thursday, Isaac and I met at a midway point between his place and mine then he took me to the shuttle bus bound for his place. A group of people was in the way so he said "Excuse me." The queue was so big, stretching down the road to the furthest minibus stop. We stood in line for half an hour until three shuttle buses came along at once. Meanwhile, some people mistook this long line for the line for other places. A couple came up and asked "Are you in the line for The Cullinan?" as they wanna be sure that it's the right queue. A burly guy cut in lind at the tail of the line. Later, a petite woman jumped the line. The svelte lady behind her said "Excuse me. The end of the line is back there." A slim guy stood beside a well-rounded and curvaceous woman. While pretending to look at his phone, edged furtively beside her who didn't realize he was there. Some people might have noticed it but ignored it. Isaac seemed on edge. It turns out he has a job interview next week. Never has he felt so on edge before an interview. Isaac's elder sister is currently a head chef at a four-star hotel and feels she's found her niche. His younger sister has also found her niche as a professor, having worked as a researcher for three years. She sometimes has a doze at lunchtime when because she has so much work to do that she feels drained. She usually naps on the sofa by the fireplace or at her desk when everyone else's eating lunch outside. She once brought her work along to the library where she dozed off at the table. Having a snooze in front of the fire is a marvelous way to replenish your energy though. His eldest sister has just completed her PhD at Peking University and is on the way to becoming a professor. China is on the way to becoming the world's strongest economy. Isaac's elder brother is robust and healthy. He does a lot of strength and conditioning exercises in his robust sneakers. After his gym sessions, he usually has a glass of robust wine and a cup of robust coffee. He lives in Japan where the economy and the political system are robust. His younger brother has opened an antique shop which created a great impression on me. There's an exquisite antique which engages my interest. I bet you the store engages lots of passers-by. I formed a favorable impression of the store. Isaac's auntie has passed away lately at the age of ninety nine. I offer my sincere condolences to his auntie's family. His friend Deborah said "My condolences on the loss of your auntie." His dad also sent his condolences to her family. His mom wrote "Our condolences go to her family." Isaac lives in the most exclusive and expensive area in Tokyo and owns many houses. Unlike Floyd, he's never ostentatious though extremely wealthy. You can find out how ostentatious Floyd's lifestyle is on YouTube, such as his car and house collection.

replies.msg: It's a good bet that you vacation in Japan over the Easter holiday since the Japanese yen has dropped fairly rapidly in the past two months. If so, I heartily recommend you take the airport express at IFC. It's very easy. Once you're at the IFC exit where you can head straight to Queen's Road Central, enter IFC, go past the escalator in front of you and keep walking until you come to another escalator. Go down it, turn left and go straight ahead. It's there. If you wNna travel to Shinjuku from Tokyo station, go through the gate, go down the escalator and look at the signs. You'll see which side is bound for Shinjuku. If you wanna have a coffee at J.Cafe, take the elevator to level four, go straight down the path and pass several shops until you come to an intersection. Turn right and It's there. If you wanna take an elevator to a hugger floor, turn left at the intersection and go past several shops until you see Pure Fitness on your left, enter Pure Fitness and it's there. I enjoy visiting the annual winter carnival where I eat sushi and go on the thrill rides, soaking up the nice Christmassy atmosphere. The sun has been dazzlingly bright so you might just as well have a good long soak in an outdoor onsen, soaking up the sun. In fact, I've just got out of an onsen, dried myself on a towel and getting dressed. However, if you ask me if Australia is a good place to go for your holidays, don't bet on it. There's nothing worth doing there. I like Japanese culture and food. I even took the JLPT at N one level. My listening far outshines my reading so the marks I gained in listening offset those I lost in reading. My friend too took the test. She's a little weak in writing but strong in speaking so the marks she lost in the writing part were offset by the marks she gained in the soeaking section. After the test, we spent money on clothes and shoes like it was going out of fashion. We went to a casino and gambled like it was going out of fashion. If you enjoy being in Japan, you might spend money on food and cosmetic products like it's going out of fashion. If you enter a casino, you might gamble like it's going out of fashion like we did.
